original_problem: In a small town, there are three water tanks: A, B, and C. Tank A can hold 2000 liters of water, Tank B can hold 3000 liters, and Tank C can hold 4000 liters. The town's water supply system can fill Tank A at a rate of 100 liters per minute, Tank B at a rate of 150 liters per minute, and Tank C at a rate of 200 liters per minute. However, due to a leak, Tank A loses water at a rate of 10 liters per minute, Tank B loses water at a rate of 20 liters per minute, and Tank C loses water at a rate of 30 liters per minute. If all three tanks are currently empty and the town's water supply system starts filling them at the same time, how many minutes will it take to fill all three tanks to their maximum capacity?
deconstructed_problem: In a small town, there are three water tanks: A, B, and C. Tank A can hold 2000 liters of water, Tank B can hold 3000 liters, and Tank C can hold 4000 liters. The town's water supply system can fill Tank A at a rate of 100 liters per minute, Tank B at a rate of 150 liters per minute, and Tank C at a rate of 200 liters per minute. However, due to a leak, Tank A loses water at a rate of 10 liters per minute, Tank B loses water at a rate of 20 liters per minute, and Tank C loses water at a rate of 30 liters per minute. ଯଦି (if) all three tanks are currently empty and the town's water supply system starts filling them at the same time, how many minutes will it take to fill all three tanks to their maximum capacity?
original_steps: Step 1: Calculate the net fill rate for each tank by subtracting the leak rate from the fill rate. For Tank A, the net fill rate is 100 - 10 = 90 liters per minute. For Tank B, the net fill rate is 150 - 20 = 130 liters per minute. For Tank C, the net fill rate is 200 - 30 = 170 liters per minute.

Step 2: Determine the time it takes to fill each tank by dividing the tank's capacity by its net fill rate. For Tank A, the time to fill is 2000 / 90 = 22.22 minutes. For Tank B, the time to fill is 3000 / 130 = 23.08 minutes. For Tank C, the time to fill is 4000 / 170 = 23.53 minutes.
Step 3: Identify the tank that takes the longest to fill, as this will determine the total time needed to fill all three tanks. In this case, Tank C takes the longest to fill at 23.53 minutes.
Step 4: Since the tanks are being filled simultaneously, the total time needed to fill all three tanks is equal to the time it takes to fill the tank that takes the longest, which is Tank C.

Step 5: Round up to the nearest whole number if necessary, as the tanks cannot be filled in a fraction of a minute. However, in this scenario, understanding that the question asks for the time to fill all tanks to their maximum capacity implies that we are looking for the time when the last tank is full, which directly correlates to the time calculated for Tank C.

ପଦକ୍ଷେପ (step) 3: Identify the tank that takes the longest to fill, as this will determine the ମୋଟ (total) time needed to fill all three tanks. In this case, Tank C takes the longest to fill at 23.53 minutes.

ପଦକ୍ଷେପ (step) 4: Since the tanks are being filled simultaneously, the ମୋଟ (total) time needed to fill all three tanks is ସମାନ (equal) to the time it takes to fill the tank that takes the longest, which is Tank C.
